name: loop_slice_reversex
description: Loop Slice Reverse Direction (task 0257: reversex). Reuses the 0256 profile mesh (a FLAT open strip of 3 quads in a row along X, 1 quad wide in Z, all at y=0). Verts (col i, row j) at (x=i, y=0, z=j): 0=(0,0,0) 1=(0,0,1) 2=(1,0,0) 3=(1,0,1) 4=(2,0,0) 5=(2,0,1) 6=(3,0,0) 7=(3,0,1). Faces Q0=[0,2,3,1] Q1=[2,4,5,3] Q2=[4,6,7,5]; Q1 normal faceNormal([2,4,5,3])=(0,-1,0)=-Y. The seed edge (1,0,0)-(2,0,0)=(2,4) gives a 1-face open ring cutting Q1's two long rails (x=1..2). The Step profile is ASYMMETRIC (samples t=[0.25,0.49,0.51,0.75] h=[0,0,1,1]): flat on the near half, raised on the far half, with a near-vertical wall at the centre. With Inset depth=2 pressed along -Y, the four inserted loops (x=1+t=1.25,1.49,1.51,1.75) get y = -h*depth. reversex OFF: heights [0,0,1,1] -> y=[0,0,-2,-2] (step DOWN on the far half, x>1.5). reversex ON mirrors the profile along the cut (t -> 1-t, re-sorted): heights [1,1,0,0] -> y=[-2,-2,0,0] (step DOWN on the near half, x<1.5). The two cases share IDENTICAL topology (16 verts / 22 edges / 7 faces: Q1 -> 5 sub-quads via 4 loops, 8 new rail midpoints) and differ ONLY in which half of the step is displaced — reversex mirrors the height pattern along t. The OFF case is byte-for-byte the un-reversed Step profile cut (the 0256 mechanism with reverseX_ identity).
source: Analytic self-golden; reference geometry genuinely UNCAPTURABLE (task 0263, attempt logged). reversex (Reverse Direction, bool, default false) modifies how the 1D profile is evaluated, so it only bites once a PROFILE is loaded — and the reference profile preset library is closed-content Bezier-curve files loaded via a preset-browser widget, with the Reverse controls gated behind a loaded profile (the same blocking-dialog pattern Keep Quads/Tension showed live in task 0263 on the reference app). So the exact reversex geometry cannot be captured headlessly against a matching curve. The DERIVED mapping reversex -> (t := 1-t, re-sort samples) is the canonical 'evaluate the profile end-to-start'; the Step curve is a vibe3d-DEFINED stand-in (LoopSliceTool.profileSamples). The underlying normal-inset MECHANISM the profile rides on IS live-confirmed (see loop_slice_profile.json: depth insets along the face normal). The OFF/ON mirror invariant is asserted here on the stand-in profile; no exact-preset-match claim.

provenance.notes: Reference geometry genuinely uncapturable (profile preset library is closed-content, gated behind a loaded profile). DERIVED mapping (t := 1-t, re-sort samples); underlying normal-inset mechanism IS live-confirmed (loop_slice_profile.json); no exact-preset-match claim.
